description: Native app surfaces phase A7 (docs/design/native-app-surfaces.md): recording on iOS simulators. Mobile contexts record the DEVICE screen through the XCUITest driver (simctl-backed startRecordingScreen, videoType h264) — no host display is involved, so device recordings never join the ffmpeg display mutex. Settings (com.apple.Preferences) is the target app for the same reasons as ios-interactions.spec.json; the generous startSurface timeout covers the cold WebDriverAgent build. SKIPs on non-macOS hosts via the platform gate.

tests.description: Record the simulator screen to .mp4 (direct write) around a find interaction — the interaction guarantees the sub-second-recording pitfall can't bite (empty payloads FAIL).

autoRecord in an iOS app-only context: the synthetic record step is pending until the first startSurface opens the simulator session, which late-starts the device recording; the end-of-context sweep saves it.
